name:
Hybrid AECO review
Create an AECO review
brief:
Hybrid AECO review
Create an AECO review demo where local users view a BIM/model workflow in-room while remote participants join through a standard collaboration route and the operator switches between views, dashboards, and markups.
Notes: hybrid meeting, professional feel, operator-led.

structure.label: CAVE
structure.description: A multi-wall shared immersive environment with strong collaborative sight lines.

suggested_structure_reason: A CAVE-style setup is a strong fit for collaborative AECO reviews because it favors shared sight lines and controlled comparison states.

assessment.recommended_route: Yes, the platform building blocks are there, but the proposed workflow still has caveats around fit, operation, or presentation. Treat this as a configuration-led workflow. Likely runtime path: Use Igloo Model Viewer or a supported BIM/model-viewer route for in-room 3D model review.; Use Content Bank entries and saved sessions to switch between prepared scenarios or media states. Likely content-prep path: Prepare the 3D model or BIM source in a format supported by the chosen model-viewer workflow.
top_dependencies: A supported 3D model, BIM viewer, or model-viewer workflow that the target Igloo environment can load.
Operator review before client-facing commitment if the answer carries ambiguity or risk.
top_unknowns: Coverage gaps by concept: local_workflow=primary, model_viewer=primary, remote_workflow=primary.
The exact model viewer, markup flow, and participant-control model still need to be chosen.
The content grouping and switching order still need to be designed for the intended audience and operator.

recommendations.title: Define the scenario states
recommendations.detail: Name the exact launch states you want, for example opener, main scene, reference panel, and reset state, so the Content Bank stays clear instead of becoming a loose media list.

recommendations.title: Stage the collaboration flow
recommendations.detail: Lead with the model first, then bring in dashboards or hybrid participation as a deliberate second beat so the review stays clear and professional.

use_case_alignment: Comparable Igloo use-case pattern: AECO teams use immersive workspaces for BIM/model review, markups, design comparison, and faster collaborative decision-making.
demo_plan_notes: Lead with one primary model route first, then bring in markups, comparison layouts, or dashboards as controlled secondary states.
For client-facing AECO demos, smooth operator flow matters more than showing every tool at once.
If hybrid participation is part of the demo, make that a planned beat rather than something always on in the background.
Client-facing immersive demos usually work best with one hero moment, two or three supporting beats, and one obvious reset path.
